ORDER DENYING WYNDHAM DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEYS FEES

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP AND WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEYS FEES. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COMPLAINT PRAYS FOR "ATTORNEY'S FEES AS ALLOWED BY LAW" WITHOUT SPECIFYING A LAW. ONE LAW PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION POINTS TO IS CCP 1021.5, UNDER WHICH ATTORNEYS' FEES ARE ADDRESSED ONLY AFTER TRIAL ("HAS RESULTED IN THE ENFORCEMENT OF AN IMPORTANT RIGHT AFFECTING THE PUBLIC INTEREST"). WHETHER ANY OTHER ATTORNEYS' FEES LAWS APPLY THUS NEED NOT BE CONSIDERED AT THIS TIME. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Wyndham Vacation Ownership Andwyndham Worldwide Corporation To File Under Seal Evidence In Support Of Its Opposition To Plaintiff Williams Motion Requesting An Order Pursuant To Civil Code
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anne Costin of Costin Law Inc. (415.977.0400) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Dylan B. Carp of Jackson Lewis (415.394.9400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Court states its views and findings in open forum. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP AND WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S Motion To File Under Seal Evidence In Support Of Its Opposition To Plaintiff Williams Motion Requesting An Order Pursuant To Civil Code 3295 motion is DENIED. The moving parties fail to meet their burden under CRC 2.550. They have not shown that there is an overriding interest in the ethics hotline complaint and related notes and have not adequately explained what proprietary interest is at stake. Plaintiff's co-employees have no privacy interest in plaintiff's description of their conduct that occurred in public. Nor is there any significant chilling effect or public policy concern because the person complaining on the hotline (the plaintiff) does not oppose public access to the information sought to be sealed and the timeshare owners' identities and personal information has been redacted. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP AND WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Prayer For Attorneys Fees is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The complaint prays for "attorney's fees as allowed by law" without specifying a law. One law plaintiff's opposition points to is CCP 1021.5, under which attorneys' fees are addressed only after trial ("has resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest"). Whether any other attorneys' fees laws apply thus need not be considered at this time. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Wyndham Vacation Ownership And Wyndham Worldwide Corporation For Order Finding Settlement Agreement Not In Good Faith And Setting It Aside As Void Because Against Public Policy
Off Calendar Apr-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anne Constin of Costin Law Inc. telephone number 415-977-0400 for Plaintiff Patricia Williams.
Dylan Carp of Jackson Lewis P.C. telephone number 415-394-9400 for Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership.
Bruce McIntosh of Berquist Wood McIntosh Seto LLP of telephone number 925-938-6100 for Defendant Anita Howell.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion or argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP and WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION'S Motion For Order Finding Settlement Agreement Not In Good Faith And Setting It Aside As Void Because Against Public Policy is denied. The motion lacks merit for at least three different procedural reasons. First, the motion is procedurally invalid because CCP 877.6 only applies to a situation where a party to a settlement agreement contends that the settlement bars indemnity and contribution claims by a non-party to the settlement against a party to the settlement. Here, no such contention is made and no indemnity or contribution claim has been asserted (or, as far as the court is aware, threatened) by the Wyndham Defendants against Ms. Howell. Nor has Ms. Howell sought (or, as far as the court is aware, stated that she desires) a determination that the settlement was made in good faith for purposes of section 877.6. Second, the motion is procedurally invalid because a determination under section 877.6 that a settlement is not in good faith does not result in setting aside the settlement and the Wyndham Defendants, as non-parties to the settlement, lack standing to seek to set the settlement agreement aside as void or for any other reason. Third, the motion is procedurally invalid because the apparent real relief sought by the Wyndham Defendants - for the trier of fact to be informed of the terms of the settlement in the event that Ms. Howell testifies at trial - is an evidentiary and/or jury instruction issue that should be decided by the trial judge, not by a pre-trial judge on a motion brought per section 877.6. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
